name: Louvre Museum Guided Tour
description: Skip the line and explore the world's largest art museum with a certified guide. See the Mona Lisa, Winged Victory, and thousands of other historic masterpieces up close.
insider_tip: Enter through the underground Carrousel du Louvre entrance instead of the main glass pyramid to bypass long queues.

name: Eiffel Tower Summit Access
description: Take the glass elevators to the very peak of Paris's iconic landmark. Experience breathtaking 360-degree views of the Seine River, Montmartre, and the sprawling Parisian cityscape.
insider_tip: Book sunset tickets 60 days in advance to watch the city transition from daylight into a sea of twinkling lights.

name: Bateaux Parisiens Seine River Dinner Cruise
description: Indulge in a premium 3-course French dinner while floating past illuminated historical monuments. Live violin and piano performances accompany the journey.
insider_tip: Ask for the Service Privilège package to get guaranteed window seating and premium champagne.

name: Palace of Versailles & Gardens Tour
description: Step into the opulent world of Louis XIV. Walk the stunning Hall of Mirrors, explore the royal apartments, and wander the vast, manicured fountains of the Grand Gardens.
insider_tip: Rent a golf cart or hop on the miniature train to save energy when exploring the massive gardens.

name: Artisanal Croissant Baking Masterclass
description: Learn the secrets of French pastry-making from a local chef in a cozy Parisian kitchen. Roll, laminate, and bake your own buttery croissants and pain au chocolat.
insider_tip: Bring an empty tupperware container so you can take home the dozen pastries you'll bake yourself.

name: Montmartre Bohemian Artists Walking Tour
description: Stroll down winding cobblestone streets, visiting historical spots frequented by Picasso and Van Gogh. Conclude with a visit to the gorgeous Sacré-Cœur Basilica.
insider_tip: Take the funicular up to Sacré-Cœur if you want to avoid climbing the 222 steep steps.

name: Le Marais Boutiques & Vintage Shopping
description: Discover high-fashion boutiques, hidden gardens, and premium vintage shops in Paris's trendiest historic quarter. A great activity for style enthusiasts.
insider_tip: Many shops are open on Sundays in Le Marais, unlike the rest of Paris where standard shops are closed.

name: Paris Catacombs Underground Exploration
description: Descend 20 meters below the streets of Paris into the historic ossuary. Learn about the city's 18th-century sanitary crisis and see millions of neatly stacked skeletons.
insider_tip: The temperature underground is a constant 14 degrees Celsius (57 F) year-round, so bring a light jacket.

name: Shibuya Sky Observatory Deck
description: Stand 229 meters above Shibuya Crossing on an open-air roof deck. Experience unmatched views of Tokyo Tower, Tokyo Skytree, and on clear days, Mount Fuji.
insider_tip: Book a slot for exactly 30 minutes before sunset to capture both golden hour and night neon panoramas.

name: Shinjuku Golden Gai Ramen & Izakaya Crawl
description: Walk the narrow alleys of Golden Gai with a culinary expert. Taste local styles of ramen, enjoy highballs, and learn Izakaya drinking etiquette.
insider_tip: Be prepared to squeeze into tiny bars that fit no more than 5 to 6 people at a time.

name: teamLab Planets Digital Art Exhibition
description: Immerse your senses in a museum where you walk through water, interact with projection-mapped flowers, and become one with infinite light gardens.
insider_tip: You must take off your shoes and roll up your pants, as some exhibits involve walking through knee-deep water.

name: Asakusa Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise-dori Street
description: Visit Tokyo's oldest Buddhist temple and stroll down a historical shopping street filled with traditional crafts, local street food, and souvenirs.
insider_tip: Try a fresh Melonpan bread from Kagurazaka or Asakusa Kagetsudo along Nakamise street; it's a warm, sweet delight.

name: Tsukiji Outer Market Sushi Masterclass
description: Source fresh fish directly from vendors in Tsukiji and learn to slice, shape, and serve premium nigiri under the guidance of a master chef.
insider_tip: Arrive hungry, as you will prepare and eat over 10 pieces of premium sushi during the course.

name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden Picnic
description: Relax in one of Tokyo's largest and most gorgeous parks. Featuring French formal, English landscape, and traditional Japanese gardens, it is a botanical paradise.
insider_tip: Alcohol is strictly banned inside Shinjuku Gyoen, making it extremely clean, peaceful, and family-friendly.

name: Akihabara Anime & Retro Gaming Hunt
description: Explore the electronic town. Visit iconic arcades, retro gaming shops like Super Potato, and anime merchandise hubs like Mandarake.
insider_tip: Arcades are usually spread over 5-6 floors, with retro machines on the top levels and modern claw machines on the bottom.

name: Mount Fuji View Hiking & Lake Kawaguchiko
description: Take an express bus to the Mount Fuji area. Hike up to the Arakurayama Sengen Park and see the iconic pagoda with Fuji in the background.
insider_tip: Check the cloud forecast on Open-Meteo or local webcams before departing; Mount Fuji is notorious for hiding behind thick cloud covers.

name: La Sagrada Familia Guided Interior Tour
description: Walk through Antoni Gaudí's unfinished cathedral. Admire the forest-like pillars and the majestic stained-glass windows that paint the interior in vibrant sunlight hues.
insider_tip: Visit in the late afternoon when the sun is low to experience the most dramatic orange and red light spills through the west windows.

name: El Born Tapas & Wine Tasting Journey
description: Explore the trendy medieval quarters of El Born, stopping at four traditional bodegas. Enjoy authentic tapas, vermouth, and regional Catalan wines.
insider_tip: Try the 'pan con tomate' and local dry-cured ham (Jamón Ibérico), which are staples of any authentic Catalan meal.

name: Park Güell Monumental Zone Walk
description: Wander around Antoni Gaudí's whimsical park. Admire the mosaic salamander, the curved serpentine bench, and the historic pavilions overlooking the sea.
insider_tip: The park is heavily sloped, so make sure to wear supportive walking shoes.

name: Gothic Quarter History Tour
description: Unravel the Roman and medieval history of Barcelona. Wander down dark alleys, visit the historical Jewish Quarter, and see the stunning Cathedral of Barcelona.
insider_tip: Look out for the hidden Temple of Augustus, featuring four massive Roman pillars tucked inside a residential courtyard.

name: Spanish Paella & Sangria Cooking Workshop
description: Shop for ingredients in the famous La Boqueria Market, then head to a private kitchen. Learn to prepare authentic seafood paella and classic red sangria.
insider_tip: Real paella uses Bomba rice and a saffron base, never chorizo. Don't mention chorizo to Catalan paella chefs!

name: Barceloneta Beach Paddleboarding & Sunbathing
description: Rent a stand-up paddleboard or simply lay on the golden sands of Barceloneta. Enjoy a refreshing swim in the Mediterranean Sea.
insider_tip: Watch out for pickpockets on the beach, and never leave your bags unattended while swimming.

name: Montjuïc Cable Car & Castle Expedition
description: Ride a scenic cable car up Montjuïc Hill. Tour the historic 17th-century military fortress at the top and capture gorgeous views of the harbor.
insider_tip: Take the cable car up, but walk down through the gorgeous Laribal gardens and the Grec theatre.

name: Tablao de Carmen Flamenco Show & Dinner
description: Experience raw, passionate flamenco in a historic village setting. Includes entry to Poble Espanyol and a drink or traditional tapas dinner.
insider_tip: Show up 45 minutes early so you can explore the open-air museum of Poble Espanyol, which is included in your ticket.

name: Broadway Theater Show Experience
description: Watch a world-class musical or play in Manhattan's legendary Theater District. Experience top-tier acting, musical scores, and set designs.
insider_tip: Visit the TKTS booth in Times Square on the day of the show to get up to 50% off same-day tickets.

name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art Tour
description: Explore 5,000 years of global art history at the Met. Key exhibits include the Temple of Dendur, armor galleries, and European masters.
insider_tip: Don't miss the Met Rooftop Garden Bar (open May to October) for amazing views of Central Park.

name: Chelsea Market & High Line Food Tour
description: Taste standard tacos, lobster rolls, and artisanal donuts inside Chelsea Market, then walk along the beautiful elevated High Line park.
insider_tip: Try the pastrami tacos at Los Tacos No. 1 inside the market; they are widely considered the best tacos in New York City.

name: Central Park Guided Bike Tour
description: Pedal past Bethesda Fountain, Strawberry Fields, Belvedere Castle, and the Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis Reservoir with an expert local guide.
insider_tip: Stick to the designated bike loop lanes, as park police actively fine cyclists riding on pedestrian walking paths.

name: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island Ferry
description: Board a ferry from Battery Park to Liberty Island. Walk the pedestal museum, then proceed to Ellis Island to discover US immigration history.
insider_tip: Take the very first ferry at 8:30 AM to beat the crowd and avoid waiting for hours in security lines.

name: Brooklyn Bridge & DUMBO Walking Tour
description: Walk across the majestic Brooklyn Bridge, learning about its engineering feats. End in DUMBO for historic cobblestone streets and waterfront views.
insider_tip: Walk from Brooklyn towards Manhattan instead of vice-versa to enjoy the skyscraper skyline directly in your line of sight.

name: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt Experience
description: Ascend 93 floors into a multi-sensory art space featuring mirrors, glass ledges, and reflecting rooms with dramatic views of midtown Manhattan.
insider_tip: Sunglasses are highly recommended due to the intense reflection of sunlight off the mirrored floors and walls.

name: Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum
description: Visit Frank Lloyd Wright's architectural masterpiece on the Upper East Side. Walk up the spiral ramp to view modern and contemporary art.
insider_tip: Take the elevator to the top floor first and walk down the spiral ramp to avoid walking uphill.

name: Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary & Ubud Walk
description: Walk among hundreds of grey long-tailed macaques in a lush jungle sanctuary containing three sacred temples covered in stone carvings.
insider_tip: Avoid making direct eye contact with the monkeys or bringing loose plastic bags, as they will try to grab them from you.

name: Tegalalang Rice Terraces & Jungle Swing
description: Admire the deep green valley of stepped rice paddies in Tegalalang. Try the famous high-altitude jungle swing for dramatic Instagram photos.
insider_tip: Arrive before 8:00 AM to enjoy the cool morning air and avoid the hordes of tourist buses.

name: Uluwatu Temple Cliff Walk & Kecak Dance
description: Tour a sea temple perched on a 70-meter cliff edge, followed by a dramatic sunset performance of the traditional Balinese Kecak fire dance.
insider_tip: Secure your glasses, jewelry, and phones before entering the temple; the local monkeys are notorious for stealing items.

name: Traditional Balinese Cooking Class in a Organic Farm
description: Pick fresh vegetables, herbs, and spices in a garden, then learn to prepare authentic dishes like Sate Lilit and Nasi Goreng using a stone pestle.
insider_tip: Ask the instructor to show you how to grind the spices using a traditional cobek (mortar and pestle) for the best flavor.

name: Tanah Lot Pilgrimage Sea Temple Tour
description: Witness one of Bali's most famous postcard landmarks: an ancient Hindu pilgrimage temple built on a unique offshore rock formation in the crashing waves.
insider_tip: You can only walk across the water to the base of the temple during low tide, so check tide charts in advance.

name: Tulamben USAT Liberty Wreck Scuba Dive
description: Dive the famous USAT Liberty cargo ship wreck, sitting just 30 meters from the beach. Swim alongside sea turtles, parrotfish, and gorgeous corals.
insider_tip: This is a shore dive, which means you walk straight into the water with your gear on. Wear booties to protect your feet from the volcanic pebbles.

name: Traditional Balinese Massage & Flower Bath
description: Rejuvenate your body and mind in Seminyak. Includes a 60-minute deep tissue massage, a body scrub, and a warm bath filled with rose and marigold petals.
insider_tip: Specify your pressure preferences (soft, medium, strong) beforehand, as Balinese massage therapists can apply very deep pressure.

name: Nusa Penida Kelingking Beach Day Trip
description: Take a speed boat to Nusa Penida island. Visit the famous T-Rex shaped Kelingking cliff, Angel's Billabong, and Broken Beach.
insider_tip: The hike down Kelingking beach is extremely steep and dangerous; wear proper shoes and only make the descent if you are in excellent physical condition.
